Title: Org Switcher Visible for Multi-Org User
Preconditions: User belongs to 2 or more organizations, logged in to storefront
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user who belongs to multiple organizations
2. [NAV] Navigate to storefront header or account area
3. [ASSERT] Locate the organization switcher control
4. [ASSERT] Verify it is visible and shows current organization name
Assertions:
[DOM] Org switcher control visible in header or account section
[DOM] Current organization name displayed on switcher
[STATE] Switcher appears only for users with 2+ org memberships
[DOM] Single-org users do not see switcher (validated in B2C-ORG-010)
Failure_Signals: Org switcher not visible for multi-org user; switcher showing for single-org users; current org name not shown on control

Title: Switch Org - Pricing Update
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B have different contract pricing for the same product
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in and confirm Org A is active (check org switcher)
2. [NAV] Navigate to a product with org-specific contract pricing
3. [ASSERT] Note the price shown for Org A
4. [ACT] Open org switcher and select Org B
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to the same product
6. [ASSERT] Verify price updated to Org B's contract price
Assertions:
[FORMAT] After switching to Org B, product price reflects Org B's contract/tier pricing
[FORMAT] Price is different from Org A's price
[STATE] Pricing update occurs without requiring page reload or re-login
[FORMAT] Cart prices also update to reflect new org's pricing
Failure_Signals: Price not changing after org switch; Org A price shown while Org B active; page reload required to see new pricing

Title: Switch Org - Cart Isolation
Preconditions: Multi-org user, logged in as Org A
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in and confirm Org A is active
2. [ACT] Add 2 specific products to cart under Org A
3. [ASSERT] Note the Org A cart items
4. [ACT] Open org switcher and switch to Org B
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [NAV] Navigate to cart
6. [ASSERT] Verify Org A cart items are NOT present in Org B cart
7. [ACT] Add a different product to Org B cart
8. [ACT] Switch back to Org A
9. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
10. [NAV] Navigate to cart
10. [ASSERT] Verify original 2 Org A items are still present
Assertions:
[DOM] Switching to Org B shows Org B's cart (not Org A's items)
[DOM] Org B cart starts empty or shows Org B's own cart
[DOM] After switching back to Org A, original 2 items still in cart
[DOM] Cart count in header reflects active org's cart
[DOM] Org B items not present in Org A cart after switching back
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A items visible in Org B cart after switch; cart shared between orgs; switching back to Org A loses original cart items

Title: Switch Org - Addresses Update
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B have different saved addresses
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as Org A
2. [NAV] Navigate to account addresses or ship-to selector
3. [ASSERT] Note the addresses available for Org A
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B using org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [NAV] Navigate to account addresses or ship-to selector
6. [ASSERT] Verify addresses shown belong to Org B
Assertions:
[DOM] Addresses after switching to Org B are Org B's addresses
[DOM] Org A addresses not shown while Org B is active
[DOM] Ship-to selector in header reflects Org B's addresses
[DOM] Checkout address options reflect active org's addresses
Failure_Signals: Org A addresses visible while Org B is active; ship-to selector showing wrong org's addresses

Title: Switch Org - Lists Scope
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B each have at least one saved list
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as Org A
2. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists
3. [ASSERT] Note lists available for Org A
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists
6. [ASSERT] Verify only Org B's lists are shown
Assertions:
[DOM] After switching to Org B, only lists associated with Org B are displayed
[DOM] Org A lists not visible while Org B is active
[DOM] Shared lists within an org are scoped to that org only
[STATE] Switching back to Org A restores Org A's lists
Failure_Signals: Org A lists visible while Org B is active; lists not scoping to active org

Title: Default Org on Sign-in
Preconditions: Multi-org user, one org configured as default
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in to storefront with multi-org user credentials
2. [ASSERT] Observe which organization loads immediately after sign-in
3. [ASSERT] Verify org switcher shows default org as active
4. [ASSERT] Verify default org's pricing and catalog are shown
Assertions:
[DOM] Default org loaded automatically on sign-in (not random or last-used)
[DOM] Org switcher displays default org name as active selection
[DOM] Default org's catalog, pricing, addresses, and cart shown
[STATE] No prompt to select org required on sign-in
Failure_Signals: Wrong org loaded on sign-in; user prompted to select org on every login; last-used org loaded instead of default

Title: Search Orgs in Switcher
Preconditions: Multi-org user belonging to 6 or more organizations
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user with 6+ org memberships
2. [ACT] Open the org switcher
3. [ASSERT] Locate the search input within the switcher dropdown
4. [ACT] Type part of an org name (first 3 characters)
5. [ASSERT] Verify results filter to matching org names
6. [ACT] Clear the search
7. [ASSERT] Verify all orgs shown again
Assertions:
[DOM] Search or filter input present inside org switcher dropdown
[STATE] Typing filters org list dynamically to matching names
[DOM] Non-matching orgs hidden while search active
[State] Clearing search restores full org list
[STATE] Selecting a filtered result correctly switches to that org
Failure_Signals: Search input missing in switcher; typing does not filter list; clearing search does not restore full list

Title: Switch Org - Navigation Reset
Preconditions: Multi-org user, currently on a deep category or account page for Org A
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as Org A
2. [NAV] Navigate to a deep category page or checkout flow
3. [ASSERT] Note the breadcrumb trail
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B using org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [ASSERT] Verify page state and navigation after switch
Assertions:
[NAV] After switching orgs, user redirected to neutral page (homepage or catalog root) rather than remaining on Org A context page
[DOM] Breadcrumbs reset to reflect Org B
[DOM] No stale Org A navigation state visible
[STATE] In-progress flows (e.g., checkout) exited cleanly
Failure_Signals: User remains on Org A context page after switch; breadcrumbs still show Org A path; stale Org A state visible

Title: Switch Back - Cart Intact
Preconditions: Multi-org user, items in Org A cart, currently viewing Org B
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as Org A and add 3 specific products to Org A cart
2. [ACT] Switch to Org B
3. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
4. [ACT] Optionally add a different item to Org B cart
5. [ACT] Switch back to Org A
6. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
7. [NAV] Navigate to cart
6. [ASSERT] Verify all 3 original Org A items present with correct quantities
Assertions:
[DOM] Switching away from Org A and back preserves Org A cart intact
[DOM] All originally added items still present after returning
[DOM] Quantities and selected variants unchanged
[DOM] Org B items not present in Org A cart after switching back
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A cart items lost after switching to Org B and back; quantities changed; Org B items mixed into Org A cart

Title: Single Org User - No Switcher
Preconditions: User belongs to exactly one organization, logged in to storefront
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user who belongs to only one organization
2. [NAV] Navigate to header and account area
3. [ASSERT] Look for org switcher control
Assertions:
[DOM] No org switcher control visible in header or account area
[DOM] Single org active by default
[DOM] UI not cluttered with switcher that has no utility
Failure_Signals: Org switcher visible for single-org user; org switcher shown but empty or broken for single-org users

Title: Org Switch Triggers White-Label Branding Update
Preconditions: Multi-org user logged in; Org A and Org B configured with different white-label branding (different logo, favicon, or theme color)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [ASSERT] Note Org A branding: logo in header, favicon in browser tab, and primary/accent theme color
3. [ACT] Open the org switcher (account menu dropdown)
4. [ASSERT] Verify org switcher shows radio buttons and search input
5. [ACT] Select Org B from the switcher
6. [WAIT] Wait for page to reload or update
7. [ASSERT] Verify logo in header has changed to Org B's logo
8. [ASSERT] Verify favicon in browser tab has changed to Org B's favicon
9. [ASSERT] Verify primary/accent theme color reflects Org B's white-label colors
10. [ACT] Switch back to Org A
11. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
12. [ASSERT] Verify Org A branding (logo, favicon, colors) is restored
Assertions:
[DOM] Header logo element src or alt text changes after switching to Org B (Org B logo displayed, not Org A logo)
[DOM] Browser tab favicon updated to Org B favicon after switch
[STATE] Theme color (CSS variables or inline styles) reflects Org B's configured brand colors
[STATE] Switching back to Org A restores Org A logo, favicon, and theme colors
[DOM] No broken image or missing logo during or after the switch
[FORMAT] Branding update occurs without requiring manual page refresh
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] logo and favicon assets load with 200 status for Org B
[CONSOLE] no 404s on Org B brand asset requests
Failure_Signals: Logo not changing after org switch; favicon unchanged; theme colors unchanged after switch; Org A logo persisting while Org B active; broken image in header after switch; branding change requiring manual refresh

Title: Org Search Bar Appears for >10 Organizations
Preconditions: Multi-org user belonging to 11 or more organizations
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user with 11+ org memberships
2. [ACT] Open the org switcher dropdown
3. [ASSERT] Verify a search bar / text input appears inside the switcher
4. [ASSERT] Verify all organizations are listed below the search bar
Assertions:
[DOM] Search input field visible inside org switcher dropdown
[DOM] Organization list displayed below search input
[STATE] All 11+ organizations visible (with scroll if needed)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] organization query returns 11+ results
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Search bar missing for user with 11+ orgs; org list truncated without search; switcher fails to open

Title: Org Search Bar Hidden for <=10 Organizations
Preconditions: Multi-org user belonging to exactly 10 or fewer organizations
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user with 10 or fewer org memberships
2. [ACT] Open the org switcher dropdown
3. [ASSERT] Verify NO search bar is shown inside the switcher
4. [ASSERT] Verify all organizations are listed directly
Assertions:
[DOM] No search input field inside org switcher
[DOM] All organizations listed directly without search
[STATE] Org count is 10 or fewer
Failure_Signals: Search bar incorrectly showing for <=10 orgs; org list missing

Title: Org Search - Boundary: Exactly 10 vs 11 Organizations
Preconditions: Two test accounts: one with exactly 10 orgs and one with exactly 11 orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as user with exactly 10 orgs
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ASSERT] Verify NO search bar displayed
4. [ACT] Log out
5. [NAV] Log in as user with exactly 11 orgs
6. [ACT] Open org switcher
7. [ASSERT] Verify search bar IS displayed
Assertions:
[DOM] 10-org user: no search input in switcher
[DOM] 11-org user: search input visible in switcher
[STATE] Boundary condition at 10/11 correctly implemented
Failure_Signals: Search bar present at 10 orgs (should not be); search bar missing at 11 orgs (should be present); inconsistent boundary behavior

Title: Org Search - Full Name Match
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs; known org name
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type full org name in search input
4. [ASSERT] Verify matching org appears in filtered list
5. [ASSERT] Verify non-matching orgs are hidden
Assertions:
[DOM] Search results show only matching organization
[DOM] Non-matching organizations hidden from list
[STATE] Search filters in real-time as user types
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] organization search returns matching results
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Full name search returns no results; non-matching orgs still visible; search not filtering

Title: Org Search - Partial Name Match
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type first 3 characters of an org name in search input
4. [ASSERT] Verify all orgs containing the partial text appear in results
5. [ASSERT] Verify orgs not containing the text are hidden
Assertions:
[DOM] Results include all organizations whose name contains the partial text
[DOM] Results filtered in real-time
[STATE] Partial match works as substring or prefix search
Failure_Signals: Partial search returns no results; only exact matches shown; search requires full name to work

Title: Org Search - Case Insensitive
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs; known org name
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type org name in lowercase
4. [ASSERT] Verify matching org appears
5. [ACT] Clear search and type in UPPERCASE
6. [ASSERT] Verify same matching org appears
Assertions:
[DOM] Lowercase search returns same results as original case
[DOM] Uppercase search returns same results as original case
[STATE] Search is case-insensitive
Failure_Signals: Case-sensitive search: lowercase fails to find uppercase org name; inconsistent results between cases

Title: Org Search - No Results Found State
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type a non-existent org name (e.g. ZZZ_NONEXISTENT_999)
4. [ASSERT] Verify empty results state is shown
Assertions:
[DOM] No organization items shown in filtered list
[DOM] Empty state message displayed (e.g. No organizations found)
[DOM] No blank or broken layout
Failure_Signals: Blank area instead of empty state message; dropdown closes when no results; JS error on empty search results

Title: Org Search - Clear Search Restores Full List
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type a search term that filters to 1-2 results
4. [ASSERT] Verify filtered results shown
5. [ACT] Clear the search input (backspace or clear button)
6. [ASSERT] Verify full organization list is restored
Assertions:
[DOM] After clearing search: all organizations visible again
[STATE] Organization count matches original full list
[DOM] Search input is empty after clearing
Failure_Signals: Clearing search does not restore full list; some orgs missing after clearing; search input not clearable

Title: Org Search - Special Characters in Query
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type special characters in search (e.g. & or apostrophe)
4. [ASSERT] Verify search handles special characters without error
5. [ASSERT] Verify matching orgs with special characters in name appear if they exist
Assertions:
[DOM] Search with special characters does not cause JS error
[DOM] Orgs with special characters in name are findable
[STATE] No XSS or injection behavior
Failure_Signals: JS error when typing special characters; search breaks with ampersand or apostrophe; XSS vulnerability

Title: Org Search - Leading/Trailing Spaces Handling
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type org name with leading and trailing spaces
4. [ASSERT] Verify matching org still appears in results
Assertions:
[DOM] Leading/trailing spaces are trimmed or ignored in search
[STATE] Results match as if spaces were trimmed
Failure_Signals: Spaces cause no-results state when org exists; search breaks with whitespace-only input

Title: Org Selection After Filtering via Search
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type partial org name to filter results
4. [ACT] Click on a filtered org to select it
5. [WAIT] Wait for page to reload or update
6. [ASSERT] Verify selected org is now active
7. [ASSERT] Verify org switcher shows new org name
Assertions:
[STATE] Clicked org becomes the active organization
[DOM] Org switcher control shows newly selected org name
[DOM] Page context updates to new org (pricing and cart)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] organization switch mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Clicking filtered org does not switch; org switcher still shows old org; page context not updating after selection from filtered list

Title: Org Search - Keyboard Navigation in Dropdown
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ACT] Type partial search to filter results
4. [KEY] Press Arrow Down to highlight first result
5. [KEY] Press Arrow Down again to move to second result
6. [KEY] Press Enter to select highlighted org
7. [ASSERT] Verify selected org is now active
Assertions:
[STATE] Arrow keys navigate through org list items
[DOM] Currently highlighted item has visual focus indicator
[STATE] Enter key selects the highlighted organization
Failure_Signals: Arrow keys do not navigate dropdown items; Enter does not select highlighted item; no visual focus indicator

Title: Org Search - Placeholder Text and Styling
Preconditions: Multi-org user with 11+ orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user (11+ orgs)
2. [ACT] Open org switcher
3. [ASSERT] Verify search input has placeholder text
4. [ASSERT] Verify search input styling is consistent with design system
Assertions:
[DOM] Search input has meaningful placeholder text
[DOM] Search input styling matches design system (font and border)
[FORMAT] Placeholder text is descriptive
Failure_Signals: Missing placeholder text; search input unstyled or inconsistent with design system

Title: Cart Isolation - Items in Org A Not Visible in Org B
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A and Org B configured; empty carts in both orgs
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user and confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to a product page
3. [ACT] Add product to cart (qty 2)
4. [ASSERT] Verify cart badge shows 2 items
5. [ACT] Open org switcher and switch to Org B
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update
7. [NAV] Navigate to cart page
8. [ASSERT] Verify Org B cart is empty
Assertions:
[DOM] Cart badge shows 0 or no badge after switching to Org B
[DOM] Cart page shows empty cart message for Org B
[STATE] Items added in Org A are not present in Org B cart
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query for Org B returns empty items array
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A cart items visible in Org B; cart badge not resetting on org switch; shared cart between organizations

Title: Cart Isolation - Org A Cart Intact After Round-Trip Switch
Preconditions: Multi-org user; product added to Org A cart
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user and confirm Org A is active
2. [ACT] Add 3 items of a product to cart in Org A
3. [ASSERT] Verify cart badge shows 3
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [WAIT] Wait for context update
6. [ACT] Switch back to Org A via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
7. [WAIT] Wait for context update
8. [NAV] Navigate to cart page
9. [ASSERT] Verify Org A cart still has 3 items of the original product
Assertions:
[STATE] Org A cart quantity preserved after round-trip to Org B
[DOM] Cart line shows same product and quantity (3) as before switch
[MATH] Cart total matches pre-switch total for Org A
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query for Org A returns original items and quantities
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Cart empty after round-trip switch; quantity changed; wrong product in cart after switching back

Title: Cart Isolation - Org-Specific Pricing in Cart
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A and Org B have different contract prices for the same product
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page and note Org A price
3. [ACT] Add product to cart
4. [NAV] Navigate to cart and note the line item price
5. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to same product page
7. [ASSERT] Verify product price reflects Org B contract price (different from Org A)
8. [ACT] Add product to Org B cart
9. [NAV] Navigate to cart
10. [ASSERT] Verify Org B cart line uses Org B pricing
Assertions:
[MATH] Org A cart line price matches Org A contract price
[MATH] Org B cart line price matches Org B contract price
[STATE] Prices are org-specific not shared
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query returns correct pricing per org context
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Same price shown for both orgs; Org A price persisting in Org B cart; cart total using wrong org pricing

Title: Cart Isolation - Coupon in Org A Not Visible in Org B
Preconditions: Multi-org user; valid coupon code for Org A
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [ACT] Add product to cart
3. [ACT] Apply coupon code in Org A cart
4. [ASSERT] Verify coupon applied and discount shown
5. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to Org B cart page
7. [ASSERT] Verify no coupon is applied in Org B cart
Assertions:
[DOM] Coupon discount visible in Org A cart
[DOM] No coupon or discount line in Org B cart
[STATE] Coupon scope is per-organization
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query for Org B shows no applied coupons
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Coupon from Org A visible in Org B cart; discount applied to wrong org; coupon leaking between org contexts

Title: Org-Specific Pricing - Contract Price for Org Member
Preconditions: Org user logged in; product has org-specific contract price different from store default
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org member
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page for {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Verify displayed price is the org contract price (not store default)
4. [ASSERT] Verify price label or badge indicates contract/special pricing if applicable
Assertions:
[DOM] Product price shown is the org-specific contract price
[STATE] Price differs from store default price
[FORMAT] Price formatted correctly with currency symbol
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] product query returns org-specific price in response
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Store default price shown instead of contract price; no price differentiation for org member; incorrect price calculation

Title: Org-Specific Pricing - Price Changes on Org Switch
Preconditions: Multi-org user; same product has different prices in Org A vs Org B
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page for {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Note Org A product price
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [WAIT] Wait for context update
6. [NAV] Navigate to same product page
7. [ASSERT] Verify price has changed to Org B contract price
Assertions:
[MATH] Org A price differs from Org B price for same product
[DOM] Price updates after org switch without manual refresh
[STATE] Product page reflects current org pricing context
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] product query returns different price after org switch
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Same price shown for both orgs; price not updating after org switch; stale price from previous org

Title: Org-Specific Pricing - Non-Org User Sees Default Price
Preconditions: Personal (non-org) user account; same product as org pricing tests
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as personal (non-org) user
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page for {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Verify displayed price is the store default price
4. [ASSERT] Verify no contract/org-specific pricing label shown
Assertions:
[DOM] Price shown is the store default (list) price
[STATE] No org-specific pricing applied for personal user
[DOM] No contract price badge or label visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] product query returns default price without org context
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org contract price shown to non-org user; wrong price tier applied; pricing context leaking from previous session

Title: Ship-To Shows Only Current Org Addresses
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A and Org B each have distinct shipping addresses
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses or Ship To selector
3. [ASSERT] Verify only Org A addresses are shown
4. [ASSERT] Verify no Org B addresses appear in the list
Assertions:
[DOM] Address list contains only addresses belonging to Org A
[STATE] Org B addresses are not visible in Org A context
[DOM] Address count matches Org A address count
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] addresses query returns only Org A scoped addresses
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org B addresses visible in Org A context; mixed addresses from multiple orgs; address scope not filtered by current org

Title: Ship-To - Switching Org Updates Address List
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A and Org B have different addresses
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
3. [ASSERT] Note Org A addresses
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [WAIT] Wait for context update
6. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
7. [ASSERT] Verify address list now shows Org B addresses (different from Org A)
Assertions:
[DOM] Address list after switch contains Org B addresses only
[STATE] Org A addresses no longer visible
[DOM] Address entries reflect Org B location data
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] addresses query after org switch returns Org B addresses
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A addresses persisting after switch to Org B; address list not updating on org switch; stale address data

Title: Ship-To - New Address Scoped to Current Org
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A active
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
3. [ACT] Click Add New Address
4. [ACT] Fill address form with test data
5. [ACT] Save the address
6. [ASSERT] Verify new address appears in Org A address list
7. [ACT] Switch to Org B
8. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
9. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
9. [ASSERT] Verify the new address is NOT in Org B address list
Assertions:
[DOM] New address visible in Org A address list after save
[STATE] New address NOT visible in Org B context
[STATE] Address is scoped to Org A only
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] addresses query for Org B does not include newly created Org A address
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: New address visible in both orgs; address not scoped to current org; address leaking across organizations

Title: Ship-To - Default Address Per Org
Preconditions: Multi-org user; Org A and Org B each have a different default address
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
3. [ASSERT] Verify Org A default address is marked with Default badge
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
7. [ASSERT] Verify Org B has its own different default address marked
Assertions:
[DOM] Org A default address has Default indicator
[DOM] Org B default address has Default indicator and is different from Org A
[STATE] Each org maintains its own default address independently
Failure_Signals: Same default address for both orgs; no default address after switch; default not persisting per org

Title: Default Org Loaded on Sign-In
Preconditions: Multi-org user with a default organization configured
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to login page
2. [ACT] Sign in with multi-org credentials
3. [WAIT] Wait for redirect to home or account page
4. [ASSERT] Verify org switcher shows the default org name
5. [ASSERT] Verify page context (pricing and addresses) reflects default org
Assertions:
[DOM] Org switcher displays the configured default organization name
[STATE] Default org is active immediately after sign-in
[STATE] No org selection prompt shown when default is configured
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] initial page load context reflects default org
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: No default org loaded; org selection prompt shown despite default configured; wrong org active after sign-in

Title: No Default Org - Org Selector Shown on Sign-In
Preconditions: Multi-org user with NO default organization configured
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to login page
2. [ACT] Sign in with multi-org credentials (no default org)
3. [WAIT] Wait for redirect
4. [ASSERT] Verify org selector/prompt is displayed
5. [ACT] Select an organization from the selector
6. [ASSERT] Verify selected org becomes active
Assertions:
[DOM] Org selection prompt or modal shown after sign-in
[STATE] User must select an org before proceeding
[DOM] After selection: org switcher shows chosen org name
Failure_Signals: No org selector shown; random org auto-selected; blank page after sign-in without default org

Title: Default Org Persists Across Sign-Out/Sign-In
Preconditions: Multi-org user with default org configured
Steps:
1. [NAV] Sign in as multi-org user
2. [ASSERT] Note the default org loaded
3. [ACT] Sign out
4. [WAIT] Wait for sign-out
5. [NAV] Sign in again with same credentials
6. [ASSERT] Verify same default org is loaded as before
Assertions:
[STATE] Default org is the same across sign-out/sign-in cycles
[DOM] Org switcher shows same org name after re-login
[STATE] Default org setting persists server-side
Failure_Signals: Different org loaded after re-login; default org setting lost; org selector shown despite default configured
